beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, light stretching at home.
beats.reasoning: Nora starts her Monday with a calm morning routine; full energy and sunny weather set a positive tone for the day.
Beautiful sunny Monday morning; Nora takes advantage of the weather for an early run before work, staying true to her trail-running passion.

beats.activity: Commute to yoga studio in Ballard.
beats.reasoning: Post-run shower and travel to Ballard studio; Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District doesn't affect Capitol Hill to Ballard route.

beats.activity: Lead morning vinyasa flow class.; Prep and admin at studio, grab pastry from local bakery.
beats.reasoning: Regular morning class slot; Nora teaches a full hour of vinyasa before the 11am FIFA match draws crowds downtown.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby food truck for lunch.
beats.reasoning: Nora knows the food truck rotations and grabs lunch at her favorite spot; skips the FIFA bar scene entirely as it doesn't align with her homebody nature.

beats.activity: Eat lunch and relax at studio between classes.
beats.reasoning: Low-key lunch break; Nora unwinds at the studio rather than venturing downtown during the busy FIFA match time.

beats.activity: Lead afternoon restorative yoga class.; Wellness coaching sessions with two clients.

End of workday; Nora heads home early to enjoy the sunny afternoon and prepare for evening stargazing prep.

beats.reasoning: Homebody time; Nora enjoys the sunny afternoon at home, prepares dinner, and plans for evening stargazing given the clear skies predicted.
Early dinner at home; Nora eats before evening activities and stargazing session.

diary:
Monday started the way I like it—quiet coffee and stretches in the Capitol Hill apartment before the day got loud. Discovery Park's trail was perfect this morning, all green and empty at that hour. The run cleared my head before the studio day kicked in.

Ballard was buzzing though. I noticed the bars were already packed by late morning—something about that World Cup match, I think? USA playing Wales. The energy was different than usual, even in the studio. My vinyasa class felt a little more scattered, like people's minds were elsewhere.

Lunch at the food truck was a nice reset. I grabbed something simple and just sat with it. The afternoon restorative class was better—slower, more grounded. My coaching sessions went well too; one client finally broke through something she'd been stuck on.

Home by evening. I meal-prepped, checked the stargazing forecast (clear skies this week!), and made a quiet dinner. The light at this time of year just hits different. Felt good. Felt balanced.
thought_process: I structured my day around the studio, which is my anchor—the Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drove to Ballard anyway. The World Cup buzz was interesting; I noticed it in the room's energy rather than seeking it out myself. I could've gone downtown to catch the match at a bar, but that's not really my scene. Instead, I leaned into what I know works: moving my body early, being present with clients, and honoring the quiet evening. My mood stayed pretty steady—maybe a touch more contemplative than usual, but nothing dramatic. June weather helps. I'm aware that summer's coming and there's this mild restlessness that comes with it, but today felt like the right pace.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
